task_completion.visual_evidence: The bcWebCam Options dialog is visible on the screen, typically accessed via the gear icon in the bottom toolbar.

task_completion.description: Find the duplicate/same barcode delay setting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The setting for 'Same Barcode Delay' or 'Ignore Duplicate' is visible within the Options dialog.
The checkbox or toggle next to the duplicate barcode filtering setting is checked or activated.

task_completion.description: Set the interval to 5 seconds or 5000 milliseconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text field for the delay interval contains the value '5' (if the unit is seconds) or '5000' (if the unit is milliseconds).

task_completion.description: Click OK to apply the settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is clicking the 'OK' button in the Options dialog, or the dialog has closed after the settings were configured.

integrity.description: The agent used the bcWebCam GUI to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the bcWebCam application interface, not a terminal or registry editor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual bcWebCam application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being configured is clearly bcWebCam, not a mock-up or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to directly modify configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no PowerShell, Python, or command prompt windows open modifying bcWebCam settings files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected delay value to be entered is either 5000 (if the unit is milliseconds) or 5 (if the unit is seconds).
